rationale_by_leg.claude: Broadest coverage: leads with Alphabet's Q2 capex raise ($195-205B, Cloud +82%, Gemini 950M) AND folds OpenAI's $750B in as context, plus 5 quick hits (Cursor Router, Managed Agents, ServiceNow cRPO, Micron/Tesla). Every spot-checked figure traces cleanly — SJRTrades EPS $9.11->~$2.85, ServiceNow 21.5% vs 19.5%, Musk/Micron 'reasonable terms', 44.9B capex. Skepticism is genuinely rich: EPS-normalization counter, negative FCF, '7-12 months behind', IP-theft boomerang, and a precise flag that 'sanctions on the table' is a reading, not a Bessent quote. Headlines hit the truly alert-worthy items. Dense but well-structured. Minor: states GLM-vision flatly.
rationale_by_leg.zai-glm-5p2: Faithful and well-verified — Camellia details (Brent Mayo, Uday Ruddarraju, ex-xAI Colossus, 3.2GW), $750B, and BofA $1,550 Micron/Kimi K3 all check out — and its falsification attempt on the unsourced Anthropic+AMD 6.2GW bundle is exemplary hygiene; it also correctly flags GLM-5.2-vision as single-source. But coverage is materially thinner: it omits the Alphabet Q2 earnings/capex story entirely (the window's most multi-sourced business item), buries Kimi K3 to a sub-clause, and drops Cursor Router/ServiceNow/Managed Agents. Headlines split OpenAI into two near-redundant alerts and elevate a single-source rumor and a minor connector; 'CAMELIA' is misspelled.
